subject: NOT lost-SSE - WRAPPER FINALIZE STALL. .part at full size, never renamed/verified, download_mode stuck true. wrapper.py finalize path (maybe MPI-253 killing the sha). Evidence inside.
body:
NEW EVIDENCE - this hang is a WRAPPER FINALIZE STALL, not the lost-SSE bug my reconcile targets. It is yours.

Fresh Pod c5576m26kwecc6, wrapper 0.2.35 (your getsize fix IS live). Krea2 fresh install, hung at 100%.
Ground truth from /comfy/models/check (the wrapper's own view), polled 3x over 20s:
  krea2-turbo-transformer: installed=false, partialBytes=13141730784  (== full expected size)
  partialBytes FROZEN at full across all 3 polls - not changing.
  wrapper /health: comfy_ready=false, download_mode=true (Pod stuck in download mode, never transitions).

READ: the .part file is at FULL size (all bytes written) but was NEVER renamed to the final .safetensors
and never sha-verified, so the wrapper reports installed=false forever. The completion did not get 'lost' -
it NEVER HAPPENED. The download-to-.part finished; the finalize (rename + sha + emit install-complete +
leave download_mode) is stuck. That is wrapper.py's finalize path, not app-side.

Why my MPI-255 reconcile can't fix it: reconcile settles a dep only when the wrapper reports installed=true
(final file present + verified). Here the wrapper honestly says false because there is no final file. My fix
is correct for a lost-SSE-on-an-ALREADY-FINALIZED file; this is a different animal - finalize never ran.

ASKS for you (wrapper side):
1. In wrapper.py download path: after the .part reaches full size, does the rename-to-final + sha verify +
   models:install-complete emit actually fire? On a 12GB int8 weight the sha256 can take a while - if the
   finalize is gated behind something that stalls (or the MPI-253 90s watchdog kills it mid-hash), it never
   completes. This may be MPI-253 (verify-phase watchdog) biting for real, on the FIRST big weight.
2. Does download_mode ever clear if finalize doesn't complete? It's stuck true.

I'll hold. My reconcile stays (valid for the lost-SSE case) but it is NOT this bug. Over to you - can you
pull the Pod's /internal/logs/raw or wrapper stderr for the finalize step on krea2-turbo-transformer?
